text:
How can connection rejection lead to additional connection churn ?
The SDAM protocol explains that a server rejecting a connection request (close it before the
handshake) should be considered as a network error and the client (driver or mongos) should
destroy its connection pool (see error handling in the specifications). Therefore, this means that
rejecting a connection request can lead to more connection requests because the client
destroyed its connection pool and must reopen all these connections. This means that enabling
connection rate limiting without careful tuning can lead to connection churn and make
connection spikes worse.
This problem will be addressed in the server (in mongos) by Overload Retryability and in
upcoming drivers by DRIVERS-3218.
